title: Japan approves the Alternate Capital Law amid strong political tensions
summary: The new legislation, which allows relocating capital functions to Osaka during emergencies, is seen as a triumph of the alliance between the government and the Ishin party.

title: Rice prices drop 10% in Japan as inventories reach a record high
summary: Stocks far exceed the adequate level, which could further pressure prices downward, according to several reports.

title: Woman arrested in Ibaraki for forcing another to sew her lips shut
summary: The victim was allegedly threatened with a needle and forced to sew her own mouth shut, an alleged act of extreme domestic violence that has shocked the nation.

title: New fraud cases at Prudential Life total 790 million yen in damages
summary: Victims already exceed 125 people and the total amount of damages amounts to 3.9 billion yen.

title: Wrestler Kirishima sees his chances of promotion to Yokozuna virtually eliminated
summary: His defeat to Anblao Seiya in the sumo tournament leaves him out of the race for the highest rank in Japanese sumo.

title: Champion Sota Fujii is one win away from earning the right to challenge for the Oza title in shogi
summary: Fujii defeated Hisashi Maruyama and prepares for the next phase of the tournament for the title.

title: Coach Hajime Moriyasu will continue to lead the Japanese national football team for six more months
summary: The Japan Football Association explained that the renewal responds to the need for stability in the face of future international commitments.

title: Iyogin financial group and Ehime Bank agree to merge by April 2027
summary: The integration will create a regional megabank with total assets of approximately 12 trillion yen.

title: Spain declares state of emergency due to wildfires affecting the outskirts of Madrid
summary: High temperatures and wind fan the flames in several parts of southern Europe, causing severe damage.

title: Writer Toshiichi Akamatsu, known for his irreverent style, dies
summary: The author debuted with the characteristic title "No fixed address or employment" and leaves a unique legacy in Japanese literature.

title: A 100-yen-per-insect campaign manages to collect more than 70,000 specimens of an invasive pest
summary: The initiative to eradicate the red-necked beetle far exceeded its goal in just a month and a half.

title: Japan approves reform of the National Referendum Law without including online advertising regulation
summary: The new law was passed in the Upper House without controls on paid internet advertisements, as opposition sectors had demanded.

title: Iwate governor recommends domestic assistance to Prime Minister Takaichi for his lack of sleep
summary: The prime minister's fatigue makes international headlines and even prompts advice for him to rest eight hours a day.

title: Vice President of the Fukuoka Assembly resigns amid a confidence scandal
summary: Nakao denied the accusations but admitted that no one trusts him, while the assembly shows its bewilderment at the resignation.

title: Court orders SK Group chairman to pay 105 billion won in historic divorce
summary: The ruling, which includes the division of shares, is one of the largest divorce settlements in South Korea.

title: Prime Minister Takaichi asks large Japanese companies to invest 250 trillion yen
summary: The government seeks a growth model driven by private investment at its annual forum with Keidanren.

title: Japan revises vaccination law to allow public use of antiviral antibodies
summary: The measure paves the way for free administration of these treatments from 2027 as part of the national immunization program.

title: Trump promises to address the South China Sea dispute at the next summit with China
summary: The White House reinforces its allied stance with the Philippines while negotiating a bilateral rapprochement with Beijing.

title: Chinese AI breakthrough triggers sharp fall in Tokyo Stock Exchange
summary: The Nikkei index plunges, dragged down by massive selling of technology and semiconductor stocks.

title: Japanese police intensify fight against transnational organized crime and scams in Cambodia
summary: A high-ranking official of the Prince G group is handed over to immigration and a yakuza member is arrested for kidnapping linked to fraud centers in Southeast Asia.
